findings.evidence: Lines 63-71: `rw [VectorFourier.fourierIntegral]`; `simp only [Real.fourierChar_apply', innerₗ_apply_apply, Circle.smul_def, Circle.coe_exp, Pi.ofNat_apply, smul_eq_mul, mul_one]`; then `rw [real_inner_comm]`, `rw [ofReal_mul, ofReal_mul, ofReal_neg]`, `ring_nf`, `simp [...]`.
findings.fix: Prove it via the local conversion lemma plus `MeasureTheory.charFun_apply`, or factor the integrand algebra into a small reusable lemma, so this theorem depends on the stated bridge rather than Circle/Fourier implementation details.
findings.issue: `fourierIntegral_one_apply` proves the pointwise formula by unfolding `VectorFourier.fourierIntegral` and a `simp only` chain through `Real.fourierChar_apply'`, `Circle.smul_def`, and `Circle.coe_exp`, then manual rewrites/ring normalization.

summary: One proof unnecessarily unfolds Mathlib’s Fourier character/Circle internals instead of reusing the higher-level characteristic-function bridge, making the normalization lemma brittle under upstream refactors.
